question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open thousands of concurrent unlimited-limit (limit=0) subscriptions via subscribeTopic() to exhaust all server-side gRPC stream threads, causing a de-facto network partition where legitimate clients receive no responses?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the race condition between disposable::dispose registration and the subscribe() call — where setOnCancelHandler is set AFTER subscribe() — to leave zombie Flux subscriptions that never terminate, accumulating until the server partitions from resource exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with no consensusEndTime and limit=0, holding a streaming connection open indefinitely, and repeat this across thousands of connections to starve the bounded thread pool and simulate a network partition for all other clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged user omit the topicID field in ConsensusTopicQuery, causing toFilter() to build a TopicMessageFilter with a null topicId, which propagates into the reactive pipeline and triggers an unhandled NullPointerException that corrupts the shared Flux state and partitions the stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: convertTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged user supply a consensusStartTime with seconds=0 and nanos=0 (epoch), causing convertTimestamp() to return 0 and triggering a full historical table scan from the beginning of time, exhausting DB connection pool resources and partitioning the service from its database?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: convertTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged user set consensusStartTime.seconds to exactly 9223372034 (one below the overflow threshold in convertTimestamp()), causing DomainUtils.timestampInNanosMax() to be called with a near-max value that overflows nanosecond arithmetic, producing a negative startTime that bypasses the isValidStartTime() check and triggers unbounded DB queries?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: convertTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged user set consensusEndTime.seconds to exactly 9223372035 (the boundary in convertTimestamp()), causing endTime to be set to Long.MAX_VALUE, which makes the stream never terminate via the takeWhile(t -> t.getConsensusTimestamp() < filter.getEndTime()) check, effectively holding the connection open forever?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user craft a ConsensusTopicQuery with consensusStartTime set to epoch and a very high limit, causing the historical Flux retrieval to emit millions of messages that overwhelm the gRPC write buffer, triggering backpressure overflow (Exceptions.isOverflow) and a DEADLINE_EXCEEDED error that disconnects the stream mid-partition?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user rapidly open and cancel (via client-side RST_STREAM) thousands of subscriptions per second, causing the setOnCancelHandler(disposable::dispose) path to fire repeatedly and overwhelm the scheduler with dispose() calls, degrading throughput to a partition-like state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged user trigger a scenario where toResponse() is called with a TopicMessage whose runningHash is a very large byte array, causing ProtoUtil.toByteString() (UnsafeByteOperations.unsafeWrap) to wrap a large buffer that is serialized into every gRPC response frame, exhausting network bandwidth and causing a partition for other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseTransactionID()] Can an unprivileged user cause a stored TopicMessage to contain a malformed initialTransactionId byte array, so that parseTransactionID() catches InvalidProtocolBufferException and logs an error for every message in a high-volume topic, flooding the logging subsystem and causing I/O saturation that partitions the service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user subscribe to a high-traffic topic with limit=0 and no endTime, then deliberately slow-read responses (by not consuming the gRPC stream), causing backpressure to build up in the Flux pipeline until an overflow exception is thrown, which via onErrorMap(ProtoUtil::toStatusRuntimeException) terminates the stream and forces a reconnect storm?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of per-IP or per-client connection rate limiting in subscribeTopic() to open connections faster than the server can register cancel handlers, leaving orphaned Disposable objects that hold DB cursors open and exhaust the connection pool, partitioning the service from its database?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with consensusStartTime far in the past and consensusEndTime far in the future (both within valid range), causing toFilter() to build a filter spanning years of data, triggering a massive DB range scan that holds a connection for minutes and partitions other queries?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user send a malformed ConsensusTopicQuery protobuf with unknown fields or deeply nested structures that cause Mono.fromCallable(() -> toFilter(request)) to throw an unexpected exception not covered by ProtoUtil::toStatusRuntimeException, resulting in an unhandled error that crashes the reactive pipeline and leaves the stream in an inconsistent state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that subscriberCount (AtomicLong) is incremented in doOnSubscribe but decremented in doFinally, and if a partition event causes doFinally to never fire (e.g., thread starvation), the counter diverges from reality, causing monitoring to miss the true subscriber load and fail to trigger circuit breakers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user send a ConsensusTopicQuery targeting a non-existent topicId with checkTopicExists=false, causing the service to create a live subscription that polls indefinitely for messages that will never arrive, accumulating idle subscriptions that consume thread and memory resources until a partition occurs?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user trigger the safety check mechanism (Mono.delay(1s).flatMapMany(_ -> missingMessages())) by subscribing to a topic during a simulated importer outage, causing repeated gap-recovery DB queries every second per subscriber, and with many concurrent subscribers, exhausting the DB connection pool and causing a partition?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged user send a ConsensusTopicQuery with limit set to Long.MAX_VALUE (max uint64), causing toFilter() to set filter.limit(Long.MAX_VALUE), which when passed to flux.take(filter.getLimit()) creates a stream that never terminates by limit, holding the connection open indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the lack of authentication or authorization on the subscribeTopic() gRPC endpoint to enumerate all valid topicIds by observing whether the response is NOT_FOUND vs. a live stream, and then flood all valid topics simultaneously to cause a coordinated partition?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user send a stream of ConsensusTopicQuery requests with alternating valid and invalid topicIds, causing the onErrorMap(ProtoUtil::toStatusRuntimeException) path to fire repeatedly and generate a high volume of StatusRuntimeException objects, causing GC pressure that degrades JVM performance to a partition-like state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: convertTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged user set consensusStartTime.nanos to Integer.MAX_VALUE (2147483647) while keeping seconds at a normal value, causing DomainUtils.timestampInNanosMax() to produce a nanosecond timestamp that overflows when added to seconds*1e9, resulting in a negative startTime that causes the DB query to scan from an unexpected offset?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the cancel handler (disposable::dispose) is only registered when responseObserver instanceof ServerCallStreamObserver, and if the gRPC framework uses a different observer implementation during a network partition event, the cancel handler is never set, leaving the Disposable running indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user open a subscription and then cause a TCP RST (by abruptly closing the connection without a proper gRPC cancel), bypassing the setOnCancelHandler path entirely, so that disposable::dispose is never called and the server-side Flux continues consuming DB resources indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/ConsensusController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged user trigger a scenario where toResponse() is called concurrently for the same TopicMessage from multiple subscribers (since there is no caching), causing CPU-intensive protobuf serialization to spike under high concurrency and degrade the server to a partition-like unresponsive state?
